With a decade of cloud architecture practice, I design enterprise-grade systems across AWS, Azure, and GCP, with particular depth in multi-cloud federation and hybrid cloud connectivity patterns. My architectural philosophy centers on explicit trade-off documentation: every significant design decision is captured in an Architecture Decision Record and reviewed with engineering and security stakeholders before implementation begins.

My infrastructure-as-code practice uses Terraform for AWS and Azure workloads and Pulumi TypeScript for dynamic resource provisioning on GCP. I design VPC networking topologies with zero-trust principles — strict IAM least-privilege boundaries, micro-segmentation, and Istio service mesh mutual TLS for east-west container traffic in Kubernetes clusters.

Reliability engineering is baked into every design: I specify RTO and RPO requirements upfront and design disaster recovery strategies accordingly, from active-active multi-region deployments for mission-critical systems to active-passive warm standby for cost-constrained workloads. FinOps governance — reserved instance planning, savings plans, and tagging policy enforcement — has delivered consistent 20-35% annual cost reductions across the workloads I oversee.
